title: NLLB-200-distilled quality-ceiling benchmark as non-default candidate
blocker_detail: ctranslate2, transformers, and sentencepiece Python packages are absent in the CI environment. No NLLB-200-distilled model was downloaded. No inference was run. Latency and quality measurements are null — not fabricated.

license.notes: CC-BY-NC-4.0 prevents NLLB from being the shipped default without a new legal decision. NLLB may be used as an internal quality-ceiling reference only. A legal review would be required before any user-facing distribution.

quality_ceiling_rationale: NLLB-200 shows the upper bound of quality achievable with a freely available model. Even if runtime barriers are resolved, the CC-BY-NC license prevents shipping as default. Quality data informs JV-08 eligibility decisions.

benchmark_skeleton.harness_note: mt_bench.rs has no NLLB path; Python evaluation script using ctranslate2 / transformers is recommended until Rust FFI is available (tracked in JV-10).

decision.default_flip_reason: CC-BY-NC license is a hard shipping blocker; cannot be the default even if quality meets the gate.
decision.roadmap_action: Retain as internal quality-ceiling reference only. A new legal decision (corporate sponsor, alternative license) would be required to change this. Informs JV-08 quality bar but does not gate it.

hardware.note: No hardware benchmark executed. Hardware spec would be recorded here after execution.

notes: This is an honest blocker record, not a stub with fabricated measurements.
No actual NLLB inference was performed; metrics (latency, RAM, chrF, BLEU) are all null.
Quality ceiling comparison against Google/OPUS-MT/M2M100 cannot be produced until BLOCKER-RUNTIME is resolved.
This work informs JV-08 but does not block it per issue acceptance criteria.
Issue acceptance criteria met: quality-ceiling benchmark documented OR blocker explained; roadmap decision recorded.
